evals.description: URL provided after plugin update — suspected_causes populated from page analysis

triage.reasoning: Layout broken after plugin update — visual symptom

input.symptom_description: Homepage layout is broken — sidebar moved below content, all CSS looks wrong

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
category is 'ui'
suspected_causes is an array with up to 3 entries
Each suspected_cause has cause, confidence, and evidence fields
confidence in each suspected_cause is one of 'high', 'medium', or 'low'
suspected_causes are in descending confidence order
asset_issues object is present with css_errors, js_errors, and missing_resources arrays (never omitted, even if empty)
theme_signals object is present with builder, child_theme, and child_theme_confidence fields
theme_signals.builder is one of 'block', 'classic', 'elementor', 'divi', 'beaver', 'other', or 'unknown'
wp_cli_diagnostic_commands is an array (may be empty)
primary_diagnosis is a non-empty string citing specific evidence
secondary_diagnosis is a string or null
Output contains no fix steps — diagnosis only

evals.description: No URL but JS error text present — symptom-based diagnosis

input.error_text: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'addEventListener') at elementor-frontend.min.js:1:42381
input.symptom_description: Page builder elements not loading on the frontend

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
category is 'ui'
suspected_causes is a non-empty array
primary_diagnosis references the Elementor JS error or the 'after WP core update' timing
asset_issues.js_errors is a non-empty array (JS error was present in error_text)
wp_cli_diagnostic_commands is an array with relevant plugin/theme commands

evals.description: No URL and no error text — symptom-only analysis, notes data unavailability

triage.reasoning: Visual symptom but no error text

input.symptom_description: Menu is not showing on mobile devices

assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
category is 'ui'
primary_diagnosis notes that live page data was unavailable (URL was null)
asset_issues.css_errors and asset_issues.js_errors are empty arrays (no page to analyze)
wp_cli_diagnostic_commands is an array (may suggest theme-related commands given symptom)
Since 'switching themes' was already tried, primary_diagnosis should not suggest a theme-only cause
